subject: hamerton
question_text: Given Hamerton's deep affection for his guardian and knowledge of her heart disease, would he choose a distant school or stay near her?
held_out: I quickly resolved that as Death would probably soon separate us, and might separate us at any moment, I would keep as much as possible near my guardian during her life.

subject: hamerton
question_text: Would Hamerton eventually master swimming after his humiliating Doncaster failure?
held_out: I afterwards became an accomplished swimmer, and could do various tricks in the water, such as reading aloud from a book held in both hands, or swimming in clothes and heavy boots.

subject: hamerton
question_text: Would Hamerton develop an interest in heraldry and medieval pursuits?
held_out: I soon learned all about heraldry, and in my leisure time drew and colored all the coats of arms that had been borne by the Hamertons... I became so much of a mediaevalist that there was considerable risk of my stopping short in the amateur practice of such arts as wood-carving, illumination, and painting on glass.

subject: hamerton
question_text: When offered a grand opportunity to organize a Lancashire art exhibition by a prominent figure, would Hamerton accept?
held_out: I felt the whole weight of my inexperience, and said to myself: 'Whatever we do it is certain that mistakes will be committed... All mistakes will be laid to my door.' ... I therefore firmly declined.

subject: hamerton
question_text: Given that Hamerton had the social credentials for a political career (old family, property, public speaking ability), would he pursue one?
held_out: A man of the world, in my position, would have found his upward course straight before him. He would simply have made use of the Church as an instrument... married for money, offered his services to the Conservative party, and gone into Parliament... In the worldly sense I never had any ambition whatever.

subject: hamerton
question_text: Would Hamerton's approach to learning French in Paris be passive or aggressive, and how quickly would he achieve fluency?
held_out: I made such good use of my time in Paris that when returning to England on the same railway, after the short interval of three months, I spoke French fluently (though not correctly) for the greater part of the way.

subject: hamerton
question_text: Would Hamerton accept or decline an invitation to attend a grand ball at the Hotel de Ville in Paris with Napoleon III present?
held_out: He therefore refused to go, and transferred his card to me; so I went with Captain Turnbull.

subject: hamerton
question_text: If placed under a tutor who treated him with contempt and tried to humiliate him, how would Hamerton respond?
held_out: He treated me with open contempt, and was always trying to humiliate me, till at last I let him understand that I would endure it no longer. One day he ordered me to clean his harness... so I answered, 'No, sir, I shall not clean your harness; that is not my work.'

subject: hamerton
question_text: Would Hamerton's time-management approach as a young artist be systematic or haphazard?
held_out: I was curiously accurate in my accounts of time, and knew to half-an-hour what was spent on this pursuit or that... 'Determined to-day to study the copper Albert Dürer 80 hours, having given 83 to the wood-cuts.'

subject: hamerton
question_text: When forced to choose between religious hypocrisy for social advancement and intellectual honesty at social cost, which would Hamerton choose?
held_out: I looked upon this obstacle as insuperable; but if I had been as indifferent on such questions as young men generally are, there would still have remained a difficulty in my own nature, which is a rooted dislike to everything which is done for social advancement.

subject: hamerton
question_text: Would Hamerton develop a strong attachment to local places where he lived, or move freely between residences?
held_out: Like all strong passions, these local attachments are extremely inconvenient, and it would be better for a man to be without them; but all reasoning on such subjects is superfluous.

subject: hamerton
question_text: Would Hamerton's adult life in France resemble or differ from his boyhood life at Hollins in Lancashire?
held_out: The sort of existence that I am leading here at Pré Charmoy, near Autun, in this year 1886, bears a wonderfully close resemblance to my existence at Hollins in 1850. I am living, as I was then, on a pretty estate with woods, meadows, pastures, and a beautiful stream.
